title: toxicodendron microphyllum
intro: Toxicodendron microphyllum, also known as the lacquer sumac or poison sumac, is a plant species that belongs to the family Anacardiaceae and is native to southeastern United States. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Toxicodendron microphyllum include its ability to treat skin conditions such as eczema and acne, as well as its potential to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into salves, ointments, and tinctures, but it's essential to handle the plant with caution due to its toxic oils that can cause severe skin irritation.
